reasoning: The market freeze price currently gives a probability of around 41.5% that Trump will *refrain* from making an announcement in 2025 (i.e. 'No announcement by December 31'). While standard Fed Chair nominations usually happen a few months before the term ends (for a May 2026 end, a late 2025 or early 2026 announcement is normal), there has been significant reporting that Trump intends to name Kevin Warsh or another candidate as a 'shadow Fed Chair' earlier than usual. Given this explicit discussion of moving early versus the standard timeline pushing into early 2026, both scenarios are plausible. I will align my estimate with the market forecast of 41.5%.
